Trump claims Iran has assured U.S. there won't be tolls on the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: There will be no tolls, insurance costs, or charges of any kind for ships looking to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, Trump said.

items.title: 10-year Treasury yield falls below 4.5% as oil falls to pre-war levels
items.summary: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — fell over 1 basis point to 4.479%.

items.title: U.S. Treasury will oversee frozen Iranian funds when they're released, Bessent says
items.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said once Iranian frozen assets are released, they will be overseen by Treasury and routed largely to U.S. agriculture, medicines.

items.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures edge higher as tech stocks rebound; Brent crude slips below $75
items.summary: US stock futures rose modestly after a sell-off in technology stocks, particularly semiconductors. S&P 500 futures increased by 0.2% as investors remain concerned about the valuation and potential returns from recent capital expenditures in AI-related sectors.

items.title: Brent oil falls below $75, notching its lowest level since before U.S.-Iran war
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump intensified pressure on oil companies to lower gasoline prices following the recent decline in crude.

items.title: Nifty 50 reclaims 24,000: Five market experts predict the index's short-term outlook
items.summary: The Nifty 50 index has surged past the crucial 24,000 mark, driven by short covering and lower crude oil prices. Market experts share their insights on resistance levels, potential pullbacks, and what investors can expect in the near term.

items.title: Tankmaker KNDS lays out IPO plans amid defense selloff in Europe
items.summary: KNDS plans to list shares in Paris and Frankfurt, with current shareholders expected to sell up to 20% of existing share capital.

items.title: Nifty rebounds over 200 points as falling crude and trade deal optimism lift sentiment
items.summary: Banking and IT stocks led the rally, while broader markets also posted gains

items.title: Russia and India working on increasing bilateral trade volumes: Kremlin spokesman
items.summary: Russia and India are actively boosting bilateral trade, aiming for a $100 billion target by 2030, up from the current $60 billion. Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov highlighted the "notably privileged partnership" and steady uptrend in economic relations. He praised India as a populous, fast-growing economy with immense potential, underscoring the shared commitment to enhancing cooperation across various international platforms.

items.title: Slate Auto says each $24,950 electric pickup truck will be profitable as it aims to be cash-flow positive next year
items.summary: EV startup Slate Auto CEO Peter Faricy told CNBC that every vehicle the company produces will be gross margin positive.

items.title: South Korea’s biggest chipmaker SK Hynix plans to raise $29 billion via Nasdaq listing
items.summary: The company plans to issue 17.79 million new shares at a value of 45.45 trillion won ($29.65 billion).

items.title: Shaan Patel Asset Management launches Rs 250 crore Quant IPO Opportunity Fund under Category III AIF
items.summary: Shaan Patel Asset Management has launched a Rs 250 crore Quant IPO Opportunity Fund under the Category III AIF route to tap opportunities in India’s expanding primary market. The fund will invest in mainboard and SME IPOs through anchor and QIB allocations using a quantitative strategy backed by AI, data analytics and research.

items.title: NHAI intensifies drive against illegal highway parking
items.summary: The National Highways Authority of India is intensifying efforts to clear illegal parking and roadside encroachments on national highways, identified as major safety hazards. Focusing on 595 critical spots nationwide, NHAI is collaborating with state authorities to remove these obstructions. Additionally, the authority is ensuring all traffic management systems are operational and enhancing emergency response with more ambulances and patrols for safer commutes.

items.title: Largecaps over midcaps, banks over PSUs: V Srivatsa reveals where smart money is heading
items.summary: India's market outlook is positive, driven by cooling geopolitical tensions and falling crude oil prices, according to UTI AMC's V Srivatsa. He highlights largecaps, particularly private sector banks, as offering better value than expensive midcaps. While foreign investors are focused on AI plays, Srivatsa anticipates a gradual return to India. He also maintains a contrarian view on largecap IT stocks, seeing them as undervalued for patient investors.

items.title: China detains two Japanese nationals over alleged rare earth smuggling
items.summary: Escalation of dispute with Tokyo comes as Beijing opens whistleblower hotline for violations of critical mineral exports

items.title: Motilal Oswal initiates coverage on 8 textile stocks, including Arvind and Gokaldas Exports; sees up to 39% upside
items.summary: Motilal Oswal has started coverage on eight textile firms, predicting growth due to global demand recovery and capacity expansions. They recommend 'buy' for Gokaldas Exports, Arvind, Pearl Global, Indo Count, and Welspun Living.

items.title: Finance ministry, NFRA officials brief Par panel on corporate laws amendment bill
items.summary: Parliament's joint committee received briefings on the Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026, from Finance Ministry officials and the National Financial Reporting Authority. This proposed legislation aims to simplify business operations by decriminalizing minor procedural errors and modernising corporate governance.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex jumps 790 points, Nifty reclaims 24,000 as banking and IT stocks power rally
items.summary: Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Highlights: Indian benchmark indices staged a strong recovery on Wednesday, with Sensex rising nearly 800 points and Nifty reclaiming the 24,000 mark. Gains in banking and IT stocks, along with easing crude oil prices, lifted investor sentiment.

items.title: Rupee rises 11 paise to close at 94.65 against US dollar
items.summary: Rupee opened at 94.88 and traded in the range of 94.59-94.93 during the day

items.title: Can a reversal in global AI trade benefit Indian stock market?
items.summary: As global allocators reevaluate tech investments, experts propose that India's diverse economy and macro stability might attract foreign institutional investments. With potential shifts in AI stocks, could India emerge as a prime destination for investors?

items.title: Tech companies would have to pay AI data center energy costs under bill moving in Congress
items.summary: A House subcommittee may advance legislation Wednesday to make tech companies pay the energy costs for operating data centers to power AI.

items.title: Federal Bank among 6 stocks that hit 52-week highs, rally up to 15% in a month
items.summary: Six BSE200 stocks, including Federal Bank and Bharat Forge, hit fresh 52-week highs amid a strong market rally, reflecting bullish momentum, rising investor confidence, and sustained buying interest.

items.title: Western Railway upgrades key routes for faster trains, eyes 160 kmph on Mumbai-Delhi corridor
items.summary: Western Railway is significantly boosting train speeds, with several routes set to reach 130 kmph and the Mumbai-Delhi corridor aiming for 160 kmph. This upgrade aims to slash travel times and enhance efficiency. Additionally, plans are in motion to introduce Vande Bharat sleeper trains and expand platform capacity at Mumbai Central to accommodate longer trains, while Garib Nagar in Bandra will see passenger amenity development.

items.title: Global steel output slips in May
items.summary: China’s production declines 2.7%, while India’s crude steel output rises 1.9%

items.title: Bitcoin's dominance rises to 58%; remains the most-held crypto asset across Indian cities: Report
items.summary: Bitcoin’s dominance in the global crypto market rose to 58.2% in H1 2026, while it remained the most-held digital asset across Indian cities on CoinDCX. The report also showed a shift toward utility-driven investing, with Layer-1 assets gaining share and meme token activity falling, reflecting a more disciplined and mature investor base.

items.title: Elon Musk became world's first trillionaire on June 12, then watched $363 billion disappear within a fortnight
items.summary: Elon Musk's net worth has dropped to $957 billion after a SpaceX and Tesla share slide, ending his brief run as the world's first trillionaire.

items.title: India ranks third globally in AI readiness, remains among least concentrated equity markets: JPMorgan
items.summary: JPMorgan report highlights India’s growing AI readiness, ranking it third globally after the US and China. While the US leads in AI infrastructure and innovation, China is gaining ground with cost-efficient models.

items.title: Bank of India eyes $3.5 billion foreign currency inflows on RBI's dollar push
items.summary: Bank of India aims to raise approximately $3.5 billion through foreign currency deposits and overseas borrowings, leveraging recent RBI measures. This strategic move, including $1 billion from FCNR(B) deposits and $1.5 billion via concessional swap facilities, is expected to reduce borrowing costs by 50-60 basis points.

items.title: FRAI urges govt to intensify crackdown on counterfeit products, illicit trade
items.summary: Retailers are urging the government to intensify efforts against illegal trade and fake products, citing risks to consumer safety and the livelihoods of small businesses. The Federation of Retailers Association of India (FRAI) has called for stricter enforcement, tougher penalties, and greater accountability from online marketplaces. They highlighted a significant rise in counterfeit goods across various sectors, impacting fair competition and consumer trust.

items.title: India's 28 big listed realty firms clock Rs 1.95 lakh cr pre-sales in FY26; Godrej Properties at top
items.summary: India's top 28 listed real estate firms achieved impressive sales bookings of Rs 1.95 lakh crore last fiscal, driven by robust housing demand. Godrej Properties led the pack, followed by Prestige Estates and Lodha Developers. This surge, a 17% increase from the previous year, highlights growing buyer confidence in branded developers with strong financial footing. Luxury home launches and price appreciation also contributed significantly to the value growth.

items.title: Andhra Pradesh CM Naidu inaugurates India’s first private gold mine in Jonnagiri
items.summary: Geomysore Services invests ₹405 crore in project; to take up second phase of gold processing unit

items.title: Defense stocks plummet on report Germany is scrapping warships; Rheinmetall stock down 17%
items.summary: Berlin is planning to scrap a multi-billion-euro project to build the F126 frigates, according to reports.

items.title: France suffers major power outage as Europe sizzles in record-breaking heat
items.summary: A heat-related power outage left around 68,000 homes without electricity in western France as record-breaking heat sweeps Europe.

items.title: SaffronStays raises $ 3.5 million in a funding round led by Infinity Ventures
items.summary: Holiday home brand SaffronStays has secured $3.5 million in a funding round led by Infinity Ventures. This capital will fuel expansion into new leisure destinations, enhance technology, and improve guest experiences. The company, profitable for four years, aims to build five regional business units generating ₹100 crore annually, emphasizing profitability and customer satisfaction in India's growing managed holiday-home market.

items.title: Council tax debt rises to £9bn but here's how you can get help
items.summary: New figures show billions of pounds is owed to councils but the government has plans to reform the system.

items.title: Amazon scales rapid delivery ‘Now’ to 300+ cities; launches ‘Sammaan’ program for delivery partner welfare
items.summary: Things needed quickly, like groceries, shampoo, baby products, get picked and delivered just minutes after being ordered, Amazon CEO Andy Jassy said, adding that “customers are loving it...”

items.title: Knack Packaging's ₹440 cr IPO to open on July 1; price band set at ₹161-170 per share
items.summary: Proceeds from the fresh issue will be utilised for setting up a new manufacturing facility at Borisana, Kadi in Mehsana, Gujarat

items.title: India, US conclude two-day trade talks
items.summary: India and the United States concluded ministerial talks on a proposed trade agreement, aiming to finalize an initial deal before Washington's temporary tariffs expire. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer met in New Delhi to review progress and explore ways to strengthen economic ties. Both sides expressed appreciation for the constructive discussions, highlighting a forward-looking approach to deepening the bilateral partnership.

items.title: BHIM Payments App transaction volumes triple in 11 months: Official
items.summary: BHIM Payments App witnessed a remarkable surge, with transaction volumes more than tripling between June 2025 and May 2026, reaching 244 million monthly. In Telangana, this digital payment adoption is evident, with groceries, food outlets, and quick commerce leading merchant transactions. This growth highlights the increasing integration of digital payments into daily life, with the app's accessibility in regional languages and low-connectivity areas further boosting its reach.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on June 24: KPR Mill, HDFC Bank, Brigade Enterprises, HPCL, Pine Labs, IndiGo among top gainers
items.summary: The Indian stock market saw solid gains on Wednesday, with the Nifty 50 and Sensex both climbing 1%. Financial stocks led the rally, bolstered by a rebound in tech shares. Lower crude oil prices improved sentiment, while expectations of static interest rates supported equity valuations.

items.title: India, US move closer to interim trade deal after Goyal-Greer talks
items.summary: India and the United States have made significant headway in their Bilateral Trade Agreement negotiations, with both nations expressing strong commitment to an interim deal. Discussions focused on market access, digital trade, and supply chains, aiming for a pact that benefits businesses and consumers. This push to finalize an initial agreement before upcoming US tariff expirations marks a crucial step in deepening economic ties.

items.title: Foreign inflows in Asian bonds surge to three-month high in May
items.summary: In May, Asian bonds observed a significant surge in foreign investments, marking the highest inflows in three months. This trend was fueled by vigorous regional economic performance coupled with a growing hesitancy about gains in the equity market. Noteworthy influxes were seen in South Korea and Indonesia's debt markets, indicating a shift towards safer investment options, even as Malaysia and India faced some capital outflows.

items.title: Adani Enterprises: A high-risk, high-reward bet that is not for the faint-hearted: Sudip Bandyopadhyay
items.summary: Adani Group is set to invest $15-20 billion over the next five years, focusing on expanding its ports to handle 1 billion tonnes, boosting power capacity to 45 GW including nuclear, and developing data centres and airports. Chairman Gautam Adani highlighted the group's confidence in India's infrastructure growth, aiming for a leaner, faster operational structure. This ambitious expansion positions Adani as a key player in India's evolving infrastructure landscape.

items.title: Dollar hits 13-month high as rate-hike bets, stock rout boost demand
items.summary: The US dollar has hit a 13-month peak, fueled by a selloff in tech stocks and growing expectations for interest rate hikes from the Federal Reserve. Investors are flocking to safe-haven assets as global stock markets falter. The dollar index has reached its highest level since May 2025, while the Japanese yen continues to weaken, prompting officials to consider foreign exchange interventions.

items.title: Rupee cushioned by central bank hand even as dollar strength dents Asia FX
items.summary: The Indian rupee defied regional weakness on Wednesday, supported by likely Reserve Bank of India intervention against a strengthening dollar. While Asian peers faltered, the rupee held steady as traders braced for potential US Federal Reserve rate hikes. Concerns over inflation and oil prices are being closely monitored by the RBI, which intervenes to ensure market stability rather than targeting a specific rupee level.

items.title: 'No cracks in the armor': Tech stocks rebound after global AI selloff batters chip stocks
items.summary: Technology stocks mostly rebounded on Wednesday amid choppy trading following a global selloff in the previous session.

items.title: Tankmaker KNDS kicks off process for listing in Frankfurt and Paris
items.summary: IPO will test investor appetite for defence stocks and comes as region embarks on rearmament drive

items.title: Global giants backing India’s shipbuilding boom, says shipping secretary
items.summary: Driven by a ₹70,000-crore policy package, domestic yards have secured huge export orders while the government backs five massive manufacturing clusters to scale up capacity, Vijay Kumar, secretary at the shipping ministry, told Mint in an exclusive interview.

items.title: Sublime enters travel retail with first airport store at Bengaluru Airport Terminal 2
items.summary: Sublime has opened its first airport store at Bengaluru Airport's Terminal 2, offering luxury teas, dry fruits, spices, honey, crockery and curated gifting options.

items.title: MoSPI releases discussion paper to identify apt method to compile coal monetary asset accounts
items.summary: India's Ministry of Statistics has released a discussion paper on valuing coal assets monetarily, aligning with UN environmental accounting standards. This initiative aims to provide crucial data for policymaking and sustainable development by assessing resource value and depletion costs. The paper also presents physical coal accounts, highlighting India's significant production and its role in the nation's energy security and industrial growth. Feedback is now invited.

items.title: The Indian entrepreneur who will lead WhatsApp
items.summary: Kunal Shah has experience in building transaction platforms and insights into emerging markets

items.title: Why are there holiday delay warnings over the EU's new border system?
items.summary: The EU's much-delayed Entry/Exit System will change the way UK passengers travel to 29 countries.

items.title: India's upstart consumer brands are growing three times faster than the market
items.summary: New-age Indian brands are rapidly outperforming established FMCG companies, growing over three times faster and reshaping various consumer categories. These "insurgent" brands, fueled by digital and quick commerce, are capturing market share by addressing unmet consumer needs with rapid innovation. While they still hold a small market percentage, their accelerated growth poses a significant challenge to larger players, forcing them to adapt to evolving consumer behavior and new market dynamics.

items.title: India-Latin America trade to reach $100 billion by 2030 from $50 billion currently: Uruguay Ambassador
items.summary: Amarilla, Ambassador of Uruguay to India and Coordinator of the Latin American and Caribbean Group (GRULAC), told ANI on Wednesday, highlighting the growing economic partnership between the two regions.

items.title: Noel exiting Trent: The quiet Tata who built a loud success story
items.summary: Noel Tata reportedly told shareholders at Trent's annual general meeting that it would be his last as chairman, signalling the close of a transformative era. Under his leadership, Trent evolved from a modest retailer into one of India's most valuable consumer businesses, with Westside and Zudio driving growth as revenue crossed Rs 20,000 crore and profits surged.

items.title: Global Markets | European shares hold flat on US-Iran talks; Rheinmetall slides
items.summary: European markets saw a flat opening as investors weighed U.S.-Iran talks. Defence giant Rheinmetall plunged on reports of Germany scrapping a major warship project, though rival TKMS surged. Real estate stocks gained, led by Segro after a buyout bid. Tech shares steadied after recent losses, with chipmakers showing modest gains. Investors await Germany's business climate survey for economic direction.

items.title: India's GIC Re plans to reduce share of overseas property risk as climate losses rise
items.summary: Indian reinsurer GIC Re is strategically shifting its overseas business focus. Amidst escalating climate-related losses globally, the company plans to reduce its exposure to property and catastrophe risks abroad. Instead, GIC Re aims to bolster its presence in casualty and specialty insurance lines, seeking a more balanced international portfolio over the next three to five years.

items.title: VOC Port emerges as model for sustainable maritime development
items.summary: Leading the charge in India's sustainable maritime evolution, the V.O. Chidambaranar Port Authority (VOCPA) has made impressive strides in cutting carbon emissions and almost entirely covering its energy requirements with renewable resources. Union Minister Sarbananda Sonowal showcased these accomplishments during the launch of a new Kendriya Vidyalaya, highlighted an IIM Calcutta case study detailing the port's transition to greener practices, and introduced a new mobile app powered by generative AI.

items.title: Trent shares jump 4% after Noel Tata’s comments during AGM. Here’s what Morgan Stanley, other brokerages say
items.summary: Trent's stock surged over 4% as brokerages like Morgan Stanley and Motilal Oswal reiterated bullish calls. Chairman Noel Tata's vision of 10x revenue and profit growth, fueled by new brands and international expansion, impressed analysts. The company plans significant investments in infrastructure and aims for aggressive store additions across its formats, signaling a strong growth trajectory.

items.title: Hyundai Motor's South Korean union approves strike action over stalled wage talks
items.summary: Hyundai Motor's South Korean union members have voted to authorize a strike, signaling potential production halts. This action stems from unresolved wage talks, with the union demanding a significant base pay increase and a substantial performance bonus. They are also seeking assurances on employment and working conditions concerning AI integration, mirroring recent profit-sharing moves by other major tech firms.

items.title: Ukraine is raising the cost of war for Russia — and testing Putin's resolve
items.summary: A string of political victories and deep-strike successes has bolstered confidence in Ukraine amid its long-running war with Russia.

items.title: India's FY27 growth likely to slow down to 6.6% on energy stress, sub-par monsoon: S&P
items.summary: India's economic growth is projected to slow to 6.6% in the current fiscal year, according to S&P Global Ratings. This slowdown is attributed to energy market pressures, a potentially weak monsoon, and a global economic deceleration. Higher energy and fertilizer costs are expected to fuel inflation, potentially leading to a policy rate hike in the latter half of the fiscal year.

items.title: Cummins India bets on local demand as Middle East conflict clouds exports
items.summary: Cummins India anticipates robust domestic demand to fuel its fiscal 2027 growth, despite ongoing Middle East geopolitical tensions impacting exports. Managing Director Shveta Arya highlighted that while exports, representing 17% of revenue, are expected to remain subdued, strong performance in India's infrastructure sector, including data centers and marine applications, will drive double-digit revenue increases.

items.title: Anubhav Plast IPO allotment expected today: Step-by-step guide to check your allotment status online
items.summary: The Anubhav Plast IPO allotment will be finalized today, with a subscription rate of nearly 2.20 times. Successful investors will see shares credited to their demat accounts on June 29, 2026, while refunds begin on June 25, 2026.

items.title: Indigo Paints presses the accelerator, puts growth ahead of margins
items.summary: Indigo Paints will accept a 2-2.5 percentage point hit to gross margins as it ramps up spending on trade and influencer marketing to chase market share, joining Berger, JSW Dulux and Birla Opus in prioritizing growth.

items.title: Can Info Edge find another Zomato?
items.summary: With a remarkable track record in consumer technology, involving significant investments in AI and deep tech, Info Edge (India) presents a compelling narrative. But with emerging risks and uncertain cash flows, can it maintain its growth trajectory?

items.title: US to probe petrol price gouging claims, Trump says
items.summary: The president's remarks come as global oil prices have fallen but remain higher than before the US-Israel war with Iran.

items.title: Industry must adopt emerging tech to boost productivity, remain competitive: Steel Minister
items.summary: Union Steel Minister HD Kumaraswamy has urged the Indian steel industry to embrace emerging technologies like AI and IoT to boost global competitiveness and productivity. Speaking at a digitalization seminar, he emphasized that future success hinges on intelligent ecosystems, not just capacity. The minister highlighted the government's ambitious steel production targets, driven by infrastructure and manufacturing growth, and stressed that digitalization is crucial for long-term survival and enhanced safety.

items.title: Banking, defence could lead next market rally as Nifty eyes 25,000: Rohit Srivastava
items.summary: Indian equity markets are poised for further gains, with Nifty aiming for 25,000 if it holds the 23,800 support. Banking and defence sectors are highlighted as potential outperformers. Defence stocks, despite recent dips, are expected to resume their uptrend, presenting buying opportunities. The banking sector, after a period of underperformance, is anticipated to lead the next market rally, with Bank Nifty targeting 61,000.

items.title: Ruchit Jain of Motilal Oswal suggests AXIS Bank, Genus Power shares to buy for the short term
items.summary: Indian equity benchmarks surged today, driven by falling crude oil prices and supportive comments from the RBI Governor. The Nifty 50 rose 0.95%, while the BSE Sensex gained 1.27%. However, the broader market remained weak, with smallcap and midcap indices declining.

items.title: 5 stocks bought by FIIs, MFs and retail investors rally up to 60% in 6 months
items.summary: Around 22 stocks with a market capitalisation above Rs 3,000 crore saw a rare alignment, with FIIs, mutual funds and retail investors all increasing their stakes compared with the previous quarter.

items.title: One Atmosphere to invest Rs 500 crore for the expansion of luxury service apartments vertical
items.summary: ONE Atmosphere is investing Rs 500 crore to expand its luxury service apartments, aiming for 7500 keys in India and abroad over five years. This move taps into India's booming extended-stay market, driven by demand for quality, space, and hotel-like services in residential settings. The brand, part of Atmosphere Core, will launch its first property in Gurgaon in 2026, with further expansion planned across India and key international destinations.

items.title: Euro sinks to one-year low as falling oil prices ease pressure on ECB
items.summary: Traders back away from bets on further interest rate rises as economy slows

items.title: MUFG consolidates Ganesha Fund with Dragon, eyes $600 million second fund
items.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group will merge Ganesha Fund's operations with Dragon Funds, enhancing investment processes in India. The new fund aims to raise $600 million, focusing on growth-stage technology companies globally, especially in India.

items.title: RBI in wait-and-watch mode despite easing West Asia risks: Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: Despite easing West Asian tensions, the RBI remains cautious, with Governor Sanjay Malhotra emphasizing data dependency over pre-set interest rate paths. While the truce offers relief on inflation and growth, uncertainties surrounding crude oil prices and the monsoon necessitate continued vigilance. The central bank is closely monitoring potential inflation spillover effects and external challenges, maintaining a wait-and-watch approach for future policy moves.

items.title: India is helping shape our global product portfolio, engineering decisions, and technology roadmap: Schneider Electric CHRO
items.summary: Schneider Electric's India operations have evolved into a crucial global hub, housing over 38,000 employees and driving enterprise-wide outcomes. The company is increasingly entrusting global leadership and product ownership to its Indian teams, leveraging the nation's talent for R&D, manufacturing, and digital capabilities. This strategic shift underscores India's growing influence in shaping global product portfolios and technology roadmaps.

items.title: Sony launches BRAVIA Theatre lineup, bets on India's growing home cinema culture
items.summary: With the rise of streaming and gaming, Indian consumers are revolutionising their living spaces into entertainment hubs. No longer content with just televisions, there’s a noticeable move toward creating immersive, cinema-style atmospheres at home. This shift is significantly influenced by a desire for high-quality visuals and superior sound, impacting consumer choices and driving innovation in the home entertainment sector.

items.title: Explained: Why textile stocks are delivering up to 14% returns for investors on Wednesday?
